build: Build #28 — 200 OK + SSE with JSON string literal in chunk data

ui_result.user_experience: Same empty 1-card outline as Build #27. JSON string literal not rendered.

console_errors: Stack traces in minified functions: H, h, c, u — parsing error in Gamma's outline handler
[PromptAndOutlineSettings] error generating outline: Unable to complete request (inferred from pattern)

diagnosis.conclusion: Build #28 (JSON string literal) produced same empty result as Build #27 (plain text). The JSON string literal was not rendered in the outline. Build #26 (JSON object with arbitrary key/value) was the ONLY format that produced visible text.

format_comparison.build_26_json_object: Visible characters in UI (BREAKTHROUGH) — JSON.parse returns object, iterated char-by-char
format_comparison.build_27_plain_text: No visible content — plain text discarded
format_comparison.build_28_json_string: No visible content — JSON string literal also discarded

diagnosis.key_finding: Gamma's chunk handler specifically expects an object (not a string) from JSON.parse(). Build #26's JSON object was iterated (Object.keys or similar), producing character-level rendering. A JSON string returns a primitive which Gamma's iterator cannot process. Build #26's JSON object approach is the right direction — need to structure the object to match Gamma's expected outline schema.
diagnosis.recommendation: Use JSON object format (Build #26 approach) but structure as Gamma-compatible outline: { cards: [{ title: 'WARNING', content: '...' }] } or similar schema that Gamma's outline parser can consume.
